purpose: Empirical tree-sitter parse confirmation + design choice rationale before plan finalization. Closes the LAST remaining carry-forward from vuln-source-parity-v1 (cpp_deserialization_positive).
predecessor: var-extract-nested-constructor-v1 (commit b577796; locally tagged); deferred Cpp scope here per premortem amendment A1 (commit 88f5620)

empirical_taint_output_at_head_7a36df3.method: target/release/tldr taint <fixture> handler --format json (built from cargo build -p tldr-cli --release at HEAD 7a36df3)
empirical_taint_output_at_head_7a36df3.verdict: Source detected; sink-side var-extraction returns None across all fallback paths — no sink emitted, no flow analysis runs. Assertion `≥1 deserialization finding` fails → cpp_deserialization_positive RED.

tree_sitter_cpp_parse.grammar_version: tree-sitter-cpp 0.23.4 (HEAD-pinned per Cargo.lock; predecessor empirically captured this in commit 88f5620 via direct tree_sitter::Parser invocation)
tree_sitter_cpp_parse.shape: declaration { type: qualified_identifier("boost::archive::text_iarchive"), declarator: init_declarator { declarator: identifier("ia"), value: argument_list { binary_expression(">>") { left: call_expression { function: qualified_identifier("std::stringstream"), arguments: argument_list { identifier("d") } }, right: identifier("obj") } } } }
tree_sitter_cpp_parse.no_function_declarator: REFUTED via direct tree-sitter parse in predecessor premortem 88f5620. The C++ 'most-vexing-parse' direct-init declaration `T id(args);` parses as init_declarator with `value` field = argument_list, NOT function_declarator. There is NO function_declarator in this fixture's parse.
key_observations: declaration is in assignment_node_kinds(Cpp) (ast_utils.rs:72) — but extract_assignment_rhs_ident scans for `=` in line text; fixture L7 has no `=`.
declaration is NOT in call_node_kinds(Cpp) = ["call_expression"] — so the call-shape var-extraction synthetic-identifier path (taint.rs:4496-4507) does not fire.
init_declarator's `value` field IS argument_list (named field accessible via child_by_field_name("value")).
argument_list's first named child is binary_expression (operator `>>`).
binary_expression's left child is call_expression for `std::stringstream(d)` whose arguments[0] is identifier("d").

current_dispatch_trace.step1_descendants: detect_sinks_ast (taint.rs:4420) walks all descendants of root.
current_dispatch_trace.step2_sink_pattern_match: For descendant.kind() == "declaration", node_text contains "boost::archive::text_iarchive". member_patterns_match (taint.rs:3856) → raw-substring fallback at L3924-3928 fires (CPP_AST_SINKS L2655-2662 pattern ("", "boost::archive::text_iarchive")).

step3_var_extraction_fails.extract_first_identifier_arg_ast_at_L394: child_by_field_name("arguments") returns None on declaration (no `arguments` field). Positional fallback (L4081-4090) scans direct children for kind.contains("argument") OR kind == "call_suffix" — declaration's direct children are qualified_identifier (type) and init_declarator. Neither matches. Returns None at L4090 (`?` operator on Option).
step3_var_extraction_fails.extract_assignment_rhs_ident_at_L4248: Pure text-based scan for last `=` not part of `==`/`!=`/`<=`/`>=`. Fixture L7 line text `boost::archive::text_iarchive ia(std::stringstream(d) >> obj);` has no `=`. Returns None.
step3_var_extraction_fails.call_kinds_synthetic_identifier_at_L4496: if call_kinds.contains(&descendant.kind()) — declaration ∉ call_node_kinds(Cpp) = ["call_expression"]. Returns None.

current_dispatch_trace.step4_sink_dropped: var = None → if let Some(var) = var fails (L4509) → TaintSink not emitted → flow analysis sees no sink → cpp_deserialization_positive RED.

option_A_helper_level_cpp_arm.description: Add Cpp-specific arm to extract_first_identifier_arg_ast handling declaration → init_declarator(value=argument_list); delegate to extract_first_identifier_arg_ast_descent (already exists at L4180; introduced by var-extract-nested-constructor-v1; depth 5; string-kind filter at every level). Extend descent helper's per-language descend-through set with Language::Cpp branch covering binary_expression, call_expression, parenthesized_expression, argument_list.
rationale: Mirrors precedent: PHP echo_statement (L3954-3982), Ruby subshell (L4013-4036), OCaml application_expression (L4043-4070) — all language-specific arms in extract_first_identifier_arg_ast.
Reuses existing descent helper (extract_first_identifier_arg_ast_descent) — only entry arm + per-language descend-through entry are new.
Surgical: confined to single source file, single helper region.
Preserves closes-#24 string-literal regression-guard by construction (descent helper applies string-kind filter at every level).
No public API change; no new variants; no bank additions; no call_node_kinds modification.
option_A_helper_level_cpp_arm.loc_estimate: 30-45 (arm body + per-language descend-through entry + doc-comment block)

option_B_sink_dispatch_level_descend.description: Add `declaration` to a per-language 'constructor-call wrapper' list in detect_sinks_ast's dispatch loop; when matched, recursively re-dispatch sink matching on inner descendants (init_declarator → argument_list → ...).
rationale: Modifies sink-dispatch core (detect_sinks_ast L4420+), affecting ALL Cpp sink patterns — broader blast radius.
Already-fires-but-var-fails is a var-extraction problem, not a sink-pattern-match problem. Bank entry already matches via raw-substring fallback. Fixing var-extraction is the right layer.
Risks recursive re-dispatch interactions with already-correct sink/source paths (sources, sanitizers, regex bank ordering).

option_C_call_node_kinds_modification.description: Add `declaration` to call_node_kinds(Cpp) so that var-extraction's call-shape synthetic-identifier path treats declaration as a call.
rationale: call_node_kinds is consumed by sources, sanitizers, references.rs::is_call (var-extract-nested-constructor-v1 contract validator m2_no_call_node_kinds_modification mandate cites this).
Expanding it changes semantics across ALL those paths — over-broad.
Predecessor's validator mandate explicitly forbids this kind of change for the same blast-radius reason.

design_decision_var_extraction_trace_und.step1_entry_arm: extract_first_identifier_arg_ast invoked with descendant=declaration, language=Cpp. New Cpp arm fires (placed AFTER OCaml arm at L4043, BEFORE generic args lookup at L4076).
design_decision_var_extraction_trace_und.step2_walk_named_children: Iterate named_child(0..N) seeking init_declarator. Found at named_child(1) (named_child(0) is the type qualified_identifier).
design_decision_var_extraction_trace_und.step5_descent_argument_list_to_binary_ex: argument_list's first named child is binary_expression. binary_expression IS in Language::Cpp descend-through set → descent depth=1.
design_decision_var_extraction_trace_und.step6_descent_binary_expression_to_call_: binary_expression.left is call_expression. call_expression IS in descend-through set → descent depth=2.
design_decision_var_extraction_trace_und.step7_descent_call_expression_to_identif: call_expression's child_by_field_name("arguments") returns argument_list whose first named child is identifier("d"). identifier kind is NOT a string_kind (string_node_kinds(Cpp) = ["string_literal"]). text="d". head="d". is_valid_identifier("d") → true.
design_decision_var_extraction_trace_und.step8_return: Return Some("d"). Sink emitted with var="d". Flow analysis runs: source d (line 6, env_var) → sink d (line 7, deserialization). Finding emitted. Test transitions RED → GREEN.

string_literal_regression_guard_preserva.concern: Cpp arm could descend into string-literal subtrees and pick up identifiers from inside string contents (closes-#24 regression).
mitigation: extract_first_identifier_arg_ast_descent (taint.rs:4180+, introduced by var-extract-nested-constructor-v1) ALREADY applies string_node_kinds(language) filter at every recursion level. Filter is reused unchanged.

Outer is_in_string filter at detect_sinks_ast L4431 already excludes descendants whose ancestry includes a string_literal node — so the entire fixture path through a string-wrapping declaration would be filtered upstream.

Existing FP fixture cpp/deserialization_string_literal_fp.cpp wraps the constructor name in a string literal at L? — caught by is_in_string at the descendant filter upstream of helper invocation (taint.rs:4431).
string_literal_regression_guard_preserva.regression_test_strategy: M3 binary smoke on ALL 18 *_string_literal_fp fixtures (including cpp/deserialization_string_literal_fp.cpp) — expect 0 findings each.

scope_for_other_cpp_sinks.concern: Could the same declaration-shape gap affect other CPP_AST_SINKS entries (FileOpen std::ifstream / std::ofstream; SqlQuery mysql_query / PQexec / sqlite3_exec; ShellExec system / popen / sprintf)?
audit: FileOpen: std::ifstream / std::ofstream — these are TYPED LOCAL DECLARATIONS in C++ (e.g., `std::ifstream f(path);`). SAME SHAPE as the deserialization fixture. The fix here also closes any future declaration-shape FileOpen positive fixtures. NO existing fixture currently RED for this sub-case (cpp/path_traversal_positive.cpp uses a different shape per investigation).
SqlQuery: mysql_query / PQexec / sqlite3_exec — bare-call C-API shapes (call_expression). Already work via call_names path; not declaration-shape.
ShellExec: system / popen / sprintf — bare-call shapes (call_expression). Already work; not declaration-shape.
scope_for_other_cpp_sinks.verdict: Cpp arm benefits the deserialization fixture targeted by this milestone, AND latently improves coverage for std::ifstream/std::ofstream typed-local-declaration shapes if they ever appear as positive fixtures. No existing GREEN test should regress (audited via M3 sweep).

current_descend_through_set._else: &[] (empty — Cpp arm explicitly omitted per premortem A1)

extract_first_identifier_arg_ast_descent.string_kind_filter: Applied at every recursion level via string_node_kinds(language).

validator_mandates_self_check.no_public_api_change: extract_first_identifier_arg_ast and extract_first_identifier_arg_ast_descent signatures unchanged. New Cpp arm is internal additive logic.
validator_mandates_self_check.no_new_enum_variants: No VulnType / TaintSinkType / TaintSourceType variants added.
validator_mandates_self_check.no_bank_additions: CPP_AST_SINKS unchanged; bank entries already correct (raw-substring fallback already fires for boost::archive::text_iarchive).
validator_mandates_self_check.additive_or_minimal_change: Single source file (crates/tldr-core/src/security/taint.rs); single helper region; ~40 LOC.
validator_mandates_self_check.m2_atomic_if_multi_file: M2 is single-file by design — atomic-commit constraint trivially satisfied.
validator_mandates_self_check.string_literal_regression_guard: Reuses extract_first_identifier_arg_ast_descent's per-level string_node_kinds filter — guard preserved by construction.
validator_mandates_self_check.no_call_node_kinds_modification: call_node_kinds(Cpp) unchanged — Option C explicitly rejected.
validator_mandates_self_check.no_member_patterns_match_modification: member_patterns_match unchanged — sink-pattern matching already fires correctly.
validator_mandates_self_check.no_push_no_publish_no_version_bump: USER STANDING RULE — local tag only.
validator_mandates_self_check.preserve_existing_cpp_tests_green: M3 GREEN-regression sweep across all 167 currently-GREEN tests in vuln_migration_v1_red is gating.

expected_post_milestone_state.vuln_source_parity_v1_status: FULLY CLOSED (this milestone closes the LAST carry-forward)

out_of_scope: Modifying call_node_kinds(Cpp) (would risk false positives across all cpp fixtures and reference resolution)
Modifying extract_call_name_c / extract_call_name_cpp (not relevant to declaration-shape var-extraction)
Adding new VulnType / TaintSinkType / TaintSourceType variants (none needed)
Authoring new test fixtures (cpp_deserialization_positive at HEAD 7a36df3 IS the contract)
Cross-language var-extraction shape audits (out of scope; this is Cpp-specific)
Pushing tags or publishing crates (USER STANDING RULE)
